channel.title: BrowBro
channel.description: In-app update feed for BrowBro. Each item is EdDSA-signed; see docs/UPDATES.md.

item.title: Version 0.1.6
item.description:
Added

  • Private windows. A new Settings → Private windows switch adds picker entries that open links in a browser's incognito/private window — per browser and even per Chrome profile ("Work Incognito" opens that profile's incognito window). Supported for Chrome, Chromium, Brave, Edge, Vivaldi, Opera, and Firefox; entries wear a sunglasses badge so a private pick is unmistakable. Safari is not supported (it offers no way to open a private window programmatically).

item.title: Version 0.1.5
item.description:
Added

  • Hold a key to pick. A new Settings → Behavior option, "Ask only when I hold a key", makes a plain click open your default browser straight away and only pops the picker when you hold a modifier while clicking. Choose the trigger key (⌘ / ⌥ / ⌃ / ⇧, default ⌥) and which browser plain clicks open in. The picker's always-on behavior is unchanged when the option is off.

item.title: Version 0.1.4
item.description:
Added

  • In-app updates. BrowBro now updates itself (via Sparkle), so DMG and Homebrew installs no longer need a manual re-download. Settings → Updates has a Check Now button and an Automatically check for updates toggle, and the menu-bar dropdown gains a Check for Updates… item. Updates are verified with an EdDSA signature and installed in place — and, unlike a manual download, they skip the first-run "Open Anyway" prompt. See docs/UPDATES.md.
  • A version indicator in Settings: the current version and build show at the bottom of the window ("BrowBro 0.1.4 (4)").
